key_uncertainties: Hideaway Beach scope and incremental capacity
timing of Hideaway addition
2022-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Hideaway Beach expansion at Perfect Day at CocoCay progressing; framed as adding an entirely new experience and expanding island capacity. CocoCay positioned alongside leading brands and innovative fleet as a core differentiator.

status_delta.detail: first quarter this asset appears in the lifecycle record

key_uncertainties: sustainability of pricing premium versus 2019
share of Caribbean guests that should ultimately visit
2022-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: CocoCay penetration of Royal Caribbean Caribbean itineraries rising to ~65% in 2023 from ~30% in 2019. Cited as a driver of business acceleration and price integrity, with land-based experiences expected to compound profitability and cash flow.

key_uncertainties: Hideaway Beach incremental daily visitors versus existing 10k baseline
transitional vs structural cost split from full-year operations
2022-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Hideaway Beach adult-only neighborhood targeted for Q4 2023, raising island capacity to 13,000 visitors daily. Direct contribution from CocoCay now visible; full-year operations cited as ~210bps of structural cost build alongside Galveston terminal. Management characterized the 2019 opening as 'ironic' and 'a huge success'.

key_uncertainties: how much of yield growth is attributable to CocoCay versus hardware and core price
structural vs transitional cost decomposition
2023-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: More than half of Caribbean sailings now visit CocoCay; called RCI's highest-rated Caribbean destination and a driver of record Caribbean yields. Q2 yield growth flagged as highest as the 2019 opening anniversary is lapped. Icon of the Seas slated to debut in Caribbean in January 2024 with CocoCay/Hideaway Beach itineraries.

key_uncertainties: sizing of the 2024 CocoCay-expansion contribution to yield alongside Icon and China reopening
trajectory of repeat-visit demand
2023-Q2.mgmt_statement_summary: Limited new disclosure beyond reiteration that full-year NCC includes ~210bps of structural cost vs 2019 mostly tied to CocoCay and Galveston operations.

key_uncertainties: margin impact of Hideaway Beach given no associated APCDs
cost comparability optics during Hideaway ramp
2023-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: Hideaway Beach launch framed as accretive to margin but distortive to cost comparisons because it carries no APCDs. Expected to lift the number of guests experiencing CocoCay.

key_uncertainties: pace at which two-thirds Caribbean penetration converts to satisfaction and margin gains
sizing of CocoCay-expansion contribution within 2024 yield bridge
2023-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Hideaway Beach completed in time for Icon of the Seas, expanding island capacity to over three million guests annually. About two-thirds of RCI Caribbean guests expected to visit in 2024. Expansion cited as one of several drivers of 2024 yield growth alongside new hardware, higher load factors, higher pricing, and commercial capabilities.

key_uncertainties: customer overlap and differentiation across CocoCay, Paradise Island, and Cozumel
2024-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Reiterated CocoCay as a driver of strong Caribbean yield growth; positioned alongside new private destinations under development as a portfolio rather than substitutes.

key_uncertainties: positioning of Paradise Island and Cozumel relative to CocoCay's customer
ticket vs onboard mix at future destinations
2024-Q2.mgmt_statement_summary: CocoCay continues to perform exceptionally well; Royal Beach Club Paradise Island (2025) and Cozumel (2026) flagged as milestones. Management noted the 2019 opening makes 1H 2024 comps easier, with adjusted yield growth roughly even across quarters versus 2019.

key_uncertainties: transferability of CocoCay economics and NPS to Perfect Day Mexico
per-guest ancillary spend at peer destinations
2024-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: CocoCay framed as the highest-NPS destination in the network and the template for Perfect Day Mexico, with paid premium experiences (Hideaway, Beach Club, slides) explicitly cited as the model to replicate.

key_uncertainties: how guest volume splits across CocoCay, beach clubs, and Perfect Day Mexico over time
incremental cost structure of Nassau relative to CocoCay
2024-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: CocoCay expected to host 3.5 million guests in 2025; positioned as complementary to Royal Beach Club with multi-stop short itineraries. Management expects Perfect Day Mexico volume to ultimately exceed CocoCay.

key_uncertainties: ticket vs onboard yield mix as Perfect Day Mexico comes online
incremental growth runway at CocoCay itself
2025-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: Some remaining growth at CocoCay acknowledged, but as Perfect Day Mexico opens the revenue contribution is expected to shift more toward a balance of ticket and onboard yield.

key_uncertainties: per-week throughput conversion into yield as itineraries layer in Royal Beach Club
2025-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Quantified weekly throughput at 24,000 guests per week through CocoCay; positioned within the multi-day proposition combining ships, CocoCay, and Royal Beach Club Nassau.

Whether Hideaway Beach's 3,000 daily visitors is incremental to the 2.5-3 million island throughput

Whether CocoCay attractiveness explains divergence between strong Caribbean demand and in-line European demand
Relative sizing of Icon, CocoCay expansion, and China reopening as 2024 yield tailwinds
Trajectory of CocoCay throughput toward 3.5M by 2025, repeat-visit demand, and Icon/Utopia deployment to the island

Attach-rate framing for Royal Beach Club Nassau using CocoCay's 3.5M annual guests as the denominator
Whether Royal Beach Clubs will replicate CocoCay's ticket-plus-onboard yield lift or shift mix toward onboard

primary_framings_observed: CocoCay as a component of the multi-driver yield bridge versus 2019
Structural versus transitional cost contribution from full-year CocoCay operations
Pricing-premium durability as island capacity expands (Hideaway Beach)
Caribbean capacity allocation and short-cruise positioning
CocoCay as the benchmark for sizing new private destinations (Paradise Island, Cozumel, Perfect Day Mexico)
Ticket-versus-onboard revenue mix at CocoCay versus successor destinations
Throughput milestones (2.5M, 3M, 3.5M annual guests; 24k per week)

currently_open_questions_as_of_2026Q1: Residual growth runway at CocoCay itself once weekly throughput is at 24k and annual guests are at 3.5M
Durability of CocoCay's pricing premium as fleetwide private-destination penetration approaches 90% by 2028
Ticket-versus-onboard yield mix at CocoCay relative to Royal Beach Clubs and Perfect Day Mexico
Quantified per-guest ancillary spend at CocoCay as a benchmark for sizing other destinations
Margin contribution of Hideaway Beach now that it is fully ramped and APCD-neutral
How CocoCay's NPS leadership translates into measurable repeat-visit and cross-sell economics
